(April 21, 1916 – October 2, 1985) was an American film and television actor.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He was notable for playing Detective Paul La Guardia in 57 episodes of the American [police procedural](/wiki/Police_procedural "Police procedural") [drama](/wiki/Drama_(film_and_television) "Drama (film and television)") *[Cagney & Lacey](/wiki/Cagney_%26_Lacey "Cagney & Lacey")* (1982–85).[[3]](#cite_note-3) He also played Detective Simms in 10 episodes of *[McCloud](/wiki/McCloud_(TV_series) "McCloud (TV series)")*.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American film and television actor

| Sidney Clute | |
| --- | --- |
| Clute in *Perry Mason*, 1958 | |
| Born | Sidney Richard Clute  (1916-04-21)April 21, 1916  [Brooklyn, New York](/wiki/Brooklyn,_New_York "Brooklyn, New York"), U.S. |
| Died | October 2, 1985(1985-10-02) (aged 69)[[1]](#cite_note-1)  [Los Angeles, California](/wiki/Los_Angeles,_California "Los Angeles, California"), U.S. |
| Occupation | Actor |
| Years active | 1946–1985 |

**Sidney Richard Clute** (April 21, 1916 – October 2, 1985) was an American film and television actor.[[2]](#cite_note-2) He was notable for playing Detective Paul La Guardia in 57 episodes of the American [police procedural](/wiki/Police_procedural "Police procedural") [drama](/wiki/Drama_(film_and_television) "Drama (film and television)") *[Cagney & Lacey](/wiki/Cagney_%26_Lacey "Cagney & Lacey")* (1982–85).[[3]](#cite_note-3) He also played Detective Simms in 10 episodes of *[McCloud](/wiki/McCloud_(TV_series) "McCloud (TV series)")*.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Clute guest-starred in numerous television programs including *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*, *[Emergency!](/wiki/Emergency! "Emergency!")*, *[The Rockford Files](/wiki/The_Rockford_Files "The Rockford Files")*, *[Kolchak: The Night Stalker](/wiki/Kolchak:_The_Night_Stalker "Kolchak: The Night Stalker")*, *[All in the Family](/wiki/All_in_the_Family "All in the Family")*, *[Adam-12](/wiki/Adam-12 "Adam-12")* and *[The Mary Tyler Moore Show](/wiki/The_Mary_Tyler_Moore_Show "The Mary Tyler Moore Show")*. He also appeared in a few episodes of *[Lou Grant](/wiki/Lou_Grant_(TV_series) "Lou Grant (TV series)")*, *[Wagon Train](/wiki/Wagon_Train "Wagon Train")*, *[Hogan's Heroes](/wiki/Hogan%27s_Heroes "Hogan's Heroes")*, *[My Three Sons](/wiki/My_Three_Sons "My Three Sons")* and *[Cannon](/wiki/Cannon_(TV_series) "Cannon (TV series)")*. Clute died on October 2, 1985, of [cancer](/wiki/Cancer "Cancer") at the St. George Hospital in [Los Angeles, California](/wiki/Los_Angeles,_California "Los Angeles, California"), at the age of 69.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)
